text: South Australia Motor Vehicles (Motor Driving Instructors and Authorised Examiners) Amendment Bill 2024 A Bill For An Act to amend the Motor Vehicles Act 1959. instructors licences 98AAI Requirement to hold motor driving instructors licence 98AAJ Application for grant or renewal of a motor driving instructors licence 98AAK Grant or renewal of instructors licence 98AAL Fit and proper person 98AAM Term of instructors licence 98AAN Instructors licence conditions 98AAO Requirement to produce instructors licence 98AAP Standards for motor driving instructors Division 3—Register of instructors licences 98AAQ Register Division 4—Code of conduct 98AAR Code of conduct for instructors licence holders Division 5—Authorised examiners 98AAS Appointment of authorised examiners 98AAT Requirement to hold appointment Division 6—Requirement for contracts 98AAU Requirement for contracts in certain circumstances Division 7—Cancellation and suspension etc of instructors licences and authorised examiner appointments 98AAV Cancellation and suspension etc of instructors licences and authorised examiner appointments Division 8—Review by defences 21 Amendment of section 145—Regulations and fee notices Schedule 1—Transitional provisions 1 Interpretation 2 Applications for motor driving instructors' licences under Motor Vehicles Act 1959 3 Current motor driving instructor's licence holders 4 Current authorised examiners 5 Crown not liable to pay compensation The Parliament of South Australia enacts as follows: Part 1—Preliminary 1—Short title This Act may be cited as the Motor Vehicles (Motor Driving Instructors and Authorised Examiners) Amendment Act 2024. 2—Commencement (1) This Act comes into operation on a day to be fixed by proclamation. (2) Section 27(6) of the Legislation Interpretation Act 2021 does not apply to this Act. Part 2—Amendment of Motor Vehicles Act 1959 3—Amendment of section 5—Interpretation (1) Section 5, definition of authority to tow—delete "or police officer" (2) Section 5(1), definition of authorised examiner, paragraphs (b) and (c)—delete paragraphs (b) and (c) and substitute: (b) a person, or a person of a specified class, appointed under Part 3A as an authorised examiner; (3) Section 5(1), definition of authorised officer—delete the definition and substitute: authorised officer means— (a) a person, or a person of a specified class, appointed as an authorised officer under section 134N; or (b) a police officer; 4—Amendment of section 43A—Temporary configuration certificate for heavy vehicle (1) Section 43A(7)(b)—delete "a police officer or" (2) Section 43A(7)(b)—delete "police officer or" 5—Amendment of section 47C—Return, recovery etc of number plates Section 47C(4)—delete ", a police officer" 6—Amendment of section 70—Return of trade plates and refunds Section 70(4)—delete "a police officer or" 7—Amendment of section 81F—Mandatory alcohol interlock scheme conditions Section 81F(1)(f)—delete "a police officer or" 8—Amendment of section 96—Duty to produce licence or permit (1) Section 96—delete "a police officer" wherever occurring and substitute in each case: an authorised officer (2) Section 96(1)—delete "the police officer" wherever occurring and substitute in each case: the authorised officer (3) Section 96(4), definition of police officer—delete the definition 9—Amendment of section 97A—Visiting motorists Section 97A(3)(a)—delete paragraph (a) 10—Amendment of section 98AAA—Duty to carry licence when driving heavy vehicle (1) Section 98AAA(1)—delete "a police officer" and substitute: an authorised officer (2) Section 98AAA(1a)—delete subsection (1a) 11—Repeal of section 98AA Section 98AA—delete the section 12—Substitution of Part 3A Part 3A—delete Part 3A and substitute: Part 3A—Motor driving instructors and authorised examiners Division 1—Preliminary 98AAH—Interpretation (1) In this Part— close associate—see subsection (4); criminal organisation means a criminal organisation within the meaning of Part 3B Division 1 of the Criminal Law Consolidation Act 1935; domestic partner means a person who is a domestic partner within the meaning of the Family Relationships Act 1975 whether declared as such under that Act or not; fit and proper person—see section 98AAL; instructors licence means a motor driving instructors licence granted under section 98AAK; relative means a person who is— (a) a spouse or domestic partner; or (b) a parent; or (c) a step‑parent; or (d) a sibling or step‑sibling; or (e) a child, step‑child or adopted child; participant, in a criminal organisation, means— (a) if the organisation is a body corporate—a director or officer of the body corporate; or (b) a person who (whether by words or conduct, or in any other way) asserts, declares or advertises their membership of, or association with, the organisation; or (c) a person who attends more than 1 meeting or gathering of persons who participate in the affairs of the organisation in any way; or (d) a person who takes part in the affairs of the organisation in any other way, but does not include a lawyer acting in a professional capacity; spouse— (a) a person is the spouse of a living person if the persons are legally married to each other; (b) a person is the spouse of a deceased person if the person was legally married to the deceased person as at the date of the deceased person's death. (2) For the purposes of this Part, a person acts as an examiner if the person conducts a practical driving test for the purposes of this Act. (3) For the purposes of this Part, a person acts as a motor driving instructor if the person teaches, coaches or instructs another person to drive a motor vehicle. (4) For the purposes of this Part, 2 persons are close associates if— (a) 1 is a relative of the other; or (b) they are in partnership; or (c) they are joint venturers; or (d) they are related bodies corporate (within the meaning of the Corporations Act 2001 of the Commonwealth); or (e) 1 is a body corporate and the other is a director, manager, secretary or public officer of the body corporate; or (f) 1 is a body corporate (other than a public company whose shares are listed on a stock exchange) and the other is a shareholder in the body corporate; or (g) 1 is a trustee of a trust and the other is a beneficiary of the trust or, in the case of a discretionary trust, an object of the trust; or (h) 1 has a right to participate, or participates, (otherwise than as a shareholder in a body corporate) in income or profits derived from a business conducted by the other; or (i) 1 is in a position to exercise, or exercises, control or significant influence over the conduct of the other. Division 2—Motor driving instructors licences 98AAI—Requirement to hold motor driving instructors licence (1) A person must not, for fee or reward, act, or offer to act, as a motor driving instructor unless the person holds a motor driving instructors licence. Maximum penalty: $20 000. (2) Subsection (1) does not apply to— (a) a person who teaches another to drive a motor vehicle if both the teacher and the learner are employed by the same employer and are acting in the ordinary course of their employment; or (b) a person, or class of persons, specified by the Registrar by notice in the Gazette. (3) A person who does not hold an instructors licence must not— (a) hold themself out as, or pretend to be, a holder of an instructors licence; or (b) use any name, title or description likely to cause any person reasonably to believe that the person holds an instructors licence; or (c) use a prescribed word, or its derivatives, to describe themself or a service that the person provides.
citation: Motor Vehicles (Motor Driving Instructors and Authorised Examiners) Amendment Bill 2024 (SA)

text: Maximum penalty: $20 000. (4) Any fee, reward or consideration paid or given to a person who has acted or offered to act as a motor driving instructor in contravention of subsection (1) may be recovered from the person as a debt. 98AAJ—Application for grant or renewal of a motor driving instructors licence (1) An application for a grant or renewal of an instructors licence must— (a) be made in a manner and form determined by the Registrar; and (b) include the information, documents or other material required by the Registrar. (2) The Registrar may, before determining an application for a grant or renewal of an instructors licence, require the applicant— (a) to provide such further information, documents or other material as the Registrar may require to determine the application; and (b) to comply with such other requirements as may be prescribed by the regulations. (3) The Registrar may, for the purpose of determining whether a person is medically fit to act as a motor driving instructor, require the person to provide such medical evidence as is required by the Registrar or undergo a medical assessment by a medical practitioner or other health professional of a class specified by the Registrar (or both). (4) The applicant may, with the approval of the Registrar or at the request of the Registrar, vary the application at any time before the application is determined. (5) The Registrar may refuse to deal with an application of a prescribed class. 98AAK—Grant or renewal of instructors licence (1) The Registrar may, on application by a person, grant or renew an instructors licence. (2) The Registrar must not grant or renew an instructors licence unless the Registrar is satisfied that— (a) the applicant is a fit and proper person to hold the instructors licence; and (b) the applicant holds an unconditional licence and has, except in circumstances determined by the Registrar, held such a licence for a minimum continuous period determined by the Registrar; and (c) is proficient to act as a motor driving instructor; and (d) the applicant holds any qualification determined by the Registrar; and (e) the applicant is medically fit to act as a motor driving instructor; and (f) the applicant complies with any other prescribed requirements. (3) The Registrar must not grant or renew an instructors licence unless the applicant has paid the prescribed fee for the grant or renewal of the licence. (4) In order to test the proficiency of an applicant to act as a motor driving instructor, the Registrar may require the applicant to undergo such tests as the Registrar may think necessary, whether written, oral or practical, and those tests must, without limiting the generality of this subsection, include examination on traffic laws, driving practices, vehicle manipulation and teaching technique. (5) Before granting or renewing an instructors licence, the Registrar must have regard to— (a) the ability of the applicant to hold a licence to act as a motor driving instructor; and (b) the safety of learner drivers and the public; and (c) any other matter prescribed by the regulations. (6) The Registrar may refuse to grant or renew an instructors licence if the Registrar determines that it would not be in the public interest to grant or renew the licence. 98AAL—Fit and proper person (1) In determining whether a person is a fit and proper person to hold an instructors licence under this Part, regard may be had to— (a) the character and reputation of the person, including the person's honesty, integrity and professionalism; and (b) whether the person is a member of, or a participant in, a criminal organisation; and (c) whether the person is a close associate of a person who is a member of a criminal organisation or is subject to a control order under the Serious and Organised Crime (Control) Act 2008; and (d) whether the person is the subject of an adverse security assessment within the meaning of the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation Act 1979 of the Commonwealth; and (e) whether the person is prohibited from working with children under section 15 of the Child Safety (Prohibited Persons) Act 2016; and (f) whether the person previously held a motor driving instructors' licence under Part 3A of the Motor Vehicles Act 1959 (as in force immediately before the commencement of section 12 of the Motor Vehicles (Motor Driving Instructors and Authorised Examiners) Amendment Act 2024) and whether that licence was at any time the subject of a suspension or cancellation. (2) A person is not a fit and proper person to hold an instructors licence under this Part if the person has been found guilty or convicted of a prescribed offence or an offence of a prescribed class. 98AAM—Term of instructors licence Subject to this Act, a licence— (a) takes effect, on grant or renewal, from the date specified in the licence for that purpose; and (b) remains in force for the period specified in the licence on its grant or renewal. 98AAN—Instructors licence conditions (1) An instructors licence may be subject to such conditions as the Registrar thinks fit and are specified in the licence. (2) Without limiting subsection (1), the conditions of an instructors licence may include— (a) limitations on the types or classes of motor vehicles that may be driven by the holder of the instructors licence, or that the holder of the licence may act as a motor driving instructor for, under the licence; and (b) limitations on the activities that may be undertaken by the holder of the instructors licence under the licence; and (c) a requirement to provide the Registrar with such information, documents or material that the Registrar may determine; and (d) a requirement that the training material provided by the holder of an instructors licence to a learner driver and the methods of training and assessment that must be used by the holder of the licence be in a form determined by the Registrar; and (e) a requirement that the person— (i) upon the request of the Registrar, provide medical evidence or undergo a medical assessment by a medical practitioner or other health professional of a class specified by the Registrar; and (ii) cover the cost of any such medical assessment and associated medical report. (3) The Registrar may at any time, by written notice given to a holder of an instructors licence— (a) vary or revoke a condition of the licence; or (b) impose a further condition on the licence. (4) A holder of an instructors licence must not contravene a condition of the licence. Maximum penalty: $15 000. 98AAO—Requirement to produce instructors licence A holder of an instructors licence must immediately produce the instructors licence when requested to do so by an authorised officer or a person for whom the holder is acting, or offering to act, as a motor driving instructor for fee or reward. Maximum penalty: $5 000. 98AAP—Standards for motor driving instructors (1) The Registrar may, by notice in the Gazette, publish standards setting out requirements to be observed by holders of instructors licences relating to— (a) designated devices, including— (i) the installation and use of 1 or more designated devices in a motor vehicle used by a holder of an instructors licence in the provision of services under the licence; and (ii) the downloading, uploading and storage of information and material on, from or in connection with designated devices, including in relation to the use of specified software or device‑based or internet‑enabled applications; and (iii) the provision of information or material (including audio, visual or audiovisual information or material) to the Registrar; and (iv) the design, service, maintenance and testing of designated devices and any associated equipment or software; and (v) any other matter related to designated devices and any associated equipment or software; and (b) motor vehicles that may be driven by holders of instructors licences, or a specified class of holders of instructors licences; and (c) any other matter the Registrar considers relevant to holders of instructors licences. (2) A standard published under this section may— (a) be of general or limited application; and (b) provide that a matter or thing in respect of which the standards apply is to be determined according to the discretion of the Registrar, or any other person determined or approved by the Registrar; and (c) provide for exemptions to be granted (with or without conditions) from the standards of a specified provision of the standards by the Registrar, or any other person authorised by the Registrar; and (d) apply, adopt or incorporate, with or without modification, any code, standard or other document prepared or approved by a body or authority referred to in the standard as in force from time to time or as in force at a specified time. (3) The Registrar may, by subsequent notice in the Gazette, vary or revoke a standard published under this section. (4) A notice published in the Gazette under this section may come into operation on the day on which it is published or on a later day or days specified in the notice. (5) If a code, standard or other document is applied, adopted or incorporated in a standard published under this section— (a) a copy of the code, standard or other document must be published on a website determined by the Registrar; and (b) in any legal proceedings, evidence of the contents of the code, standard or other document may be given by production of a document apparently certified by or on behalf of the Registrar as a true copy of the code, standard or other document. (6) Information or material (including audio, visual or audiovisual information or material) derived from the use of a designated device and provided to the Registrar in accordance with this Act is the property of the Crown. (7) Information or material referred to in subsection (6) may be used— (a) for the purposes of an audit conducted under section 98AAX; and (b) in connection with the investigation of a suspected offence against this Act or any other Act; and (c) in evidence in civil or criminal proceedings against any person (including the person who provided the information or material); and (d) for any other purpose prescribed by the regulations. (8) A person who contravenes a standard published under this section is guilty of an offence.
citation: Motor Vehicles (Motor Driving Instructors and Authorised Examiners) Amendment Bill 2024 (SA)

text: Maximum penalty: $15 000. (9) No civil or criminal liability attaches to the Crown or the Registrar in respect of any act or omission in connection with— (a) the exercise or discharge, or purported exercise or discharge, of a power or function under this section (including under a standard published under this section); or (b) the carrying out, or purported carrying out, of any requirement given or imposed, or purportedly given or imposed, under a standard published under this section. (10) Sections 10 (other than subsection (1)) and 10A of the Legislative Instruments Act 1978 apply to a notice published under subsection (1) or (3) (and a reference in those provisions to a regulation will be taken to be a reference to a notice published under subsection (1) or (3) (as the case requires)). (11) In this section— designated device means a camera, GPS tracking device or any other device, equipment or vehicle fitting determined (from time to time) by the Registrar by notice in the Gazette. Division 3—Register of instructors licences 98AAQ—Register (1) The Registrar must keep a register of instructors licences. (2) The register— (a) must include, in respect of each person who holds an instructors licence— (i) the name of the person; and (ii) the contact details of the person; and (iii) the conditions (if any) of the instructors licence; and (iv) any disciplinary action taken against the person under section 98AAV; and (v) if the instructors licence is suspended—that fact; and (vi) prescribed information; and (b) may include such other information as the Registrar thinks fit to include. (3) The Registrar may, at any time, alter information entered in the register in respect of an instructors licence to ensure that the register is accurate. (4) The Registrar must make the information referred to in subsection (2)(a)(i), (iii), (iv) and (v) available for inspection by the public on a website maintained by the Registrar or by other electronic means. (5) Information referred to in subsection (2)(a)(iv) is not required to be made publicly available if the Registrar considers that it should not be published due to the age of the information or for any other reason determined by the Registrar. (6) No civil or criminal liability attaches to the Crown or the Registrar in respect of any act or omission in connection with the maintenance of the register. Division 4—Code of conduct 98AAR—Code of conduct for instructors licence holders (1) For the purposes of this Part, the Registrar may, by notice in the Gazette, publish a code of conduct to be observed by persons who hold instructors licences. (2) Without limiting the matters that may be included in a code of conduct, a code of conduct may include measures that can reasonably be considered appropriate and adapted to promote the safety and protection of learner drivers. (3) A code of conduct may— (a) be of general or limited application; and (b) vary in operation according to factors stated in the code; and (c) provide for the granting by the Registrar of exemptions (conditional or unconditional) from specified provisions of the code; and (d) apply, adopt or incorporate, with or without modification, any code, standard or other document prepared or approved by a body or authority referred to in the code of conduct as in force from time to time or as in force at a specified time. (4) If a code, standard or other document is applied, adopted or incorporated in a code of conduct— (a) a copy of the code, standard or other document must be published on a website determined by the Registrar; and (b) in any legal proceedings, evidence of the contents of the code, standard or other document may be given by production of a document apparently certified by or on behalf of the Registrar as a true copy of the code, standard or other document. (5) A notice published in the Gazette under this section may come into operation on the day on which it is published or on a later day or days specified in the notice. (6) It is a condition of every instructors licence that the holder of the licence not contravene a code of conduct. Division 5—Authorised examiners 98AAS—Appointment of authorised examiners (1) The Registrar may, by instrument in writing, appoint— (a) a specified person to be an authorised examiner; or (b) persons of a specified class to be authorised examiners. (2) The following provisions apply to an appointment of a person under subsection (1): (a) the Registrar may require the payment of a prescribed fee before making the appointment; (b) the appointment may be made subject to conditions or limitations specified in the instrument of appointment. 98AAT—Requirement to hold appointment (1) A person must not, for fee or reward, act, or offer to act, as an examiner unless the person holds an appointment as an authorised examiner. Maximum penalty: $20 000. (2) Subsection (1) does not apply to a person, or class of persons, specified by the Registrar by notice in the Gazette. (3) A person who does not hold an appointment as an authorised examiner must not— (a) hold themself out as, or pretend to be, an authorised examiner; or (b) use any name, title or description likely to cause any person reasonably to believe that the person holds an appointment; or (c) use a prescribed word, or its derivatives, to describe themself or a service that the person provides.
citation: Motor Vehicles (Motor Driving Instructors and Authorised Examiners) Amendment Bill 2024 (SA)

text: Maximum penalty: $20 000. (4) Any fee, reward or consideration paid or given to a person who has acted or offered to act as an examiner in contravention of subsection (1) may be recovered from the person as a debt. Division 6—Requirement for contracts 98AAU—Requirement for contracts in certain circumstances (1) A holder of an instructors licence must, before acting as a motor driving instructor for a person for fee or reward, enter into a written contract with the person. (2) Before an authorised examiner acts as an examiner for fee or reward for a person, the authorised examiner, or the entity that employs or engages the authorised examiner and provides the authorised examiner to the person for the purposes of acting as an examiner, must, subject to the regulations, enter into a written contract with the person. (3) A written contract entered into for the purposes of this section must comply with any requirements determined by the Registrar. (4) A person who contravenes this section is guilty of an offence. Maximum penalty: $5 000.
citation: Motor Vehicles (Motor Driving Instructors and Authorised Examiners) Amendment Bill 2024 (SA)

text: Division 7—Cancellation and suspension etc of instructors licences and authorised examiner appointments 98AAV—Cancellation and suspension etc of instructors licences and authorised examiner appointments (1) If the Registrar is satisfied that there is proper cause for disciplinary action against a holder of an instructors licence or a person appointed as an authorised examiner, the Registrar may— (a) suspend the licence or appointment; or (b) cancel the licence or appointment; or (c) vary or revoke a condition of the licence or appointment; or (d) impose a further condition on the licence or appointment. (2) If the Registrar cancels a person's instructors licence or appointment as an authorised examiner under subsection (1), the Registrar may also disqualify the person from holding an instructors licence or appointment (as the case requires)— (a) permanently; or (b) for a specified period; or (c) until the fulfilment of specified conditions; or (d) until further order of the Registrar. (3) There is proper cause for disciplinary action against a holder of an instructors licence or a person appointed as an authorised examiner if the Registrar is satisfied that the licence holder or person appointed as an authorised examiner— (a) has failed to pay any fee or charge payable to the Registrar within the required time; or (b) has contravened this Act or any other Act or law (including an Act or law of another Australian jurisdiction), that, in the opinion of the Registrar, justifies action to be taken under this section; or (c) is charged with an offence under this Part or any other offence prescribed by regulation; or (d) has failed to maintain insurance to a level or amount determined by the Registrar; or (e) has contravened a condition of the licence or appointment; or (f) has contravened a standard published under section 98AAP; or (g) the licence or appointment was obtained improperly; or (h) in the case of a holder of an instructors licence—is no longer a fit and proper person to hold the licence. (4) A suspension may be imposed for a specified period, or until the fulfilment of specified conditions, or until further order of the Registrar. (5) A suspension may be expressed to have effect at a specified future time, or to have effect at a specified future time unless a specified condition is fulfilled. (6) Before the Registrar acts under this section, the Registrar must— (a) give the holder of an instructors licence or person appointed as an authorised examiner notice of the proposed disciplinary action specifying the reasons for the proposed action; and (b) allow the licence holder or person appointed as an authorised examiner at least 14 days within which to make submissions to the Registrar in relation to the proposed action. (7) Despite subsection (6), the Registrar may immediately suspend an instructors licence or appointment as an authorised examiner if the Registrar considers that it is in the public interest to do so. (8) If the Registrar suspends an instructors licence or appointment under subsection (7), the Registrar must give the holder of the licence or person appointed as an authorised examiner notice of the suspension as soon as is reasonably practicable after suspending the licence or appointment. (9) If the Registrar suspends or cancels an instructors licence or appointment and requests the holder of the licence or person appointed as an authorised examiner to return the licence or instrument of appointment to the Registrar, the licence holder or person appointed as an authorised examiner must do so within 14 days of the request. Maximum penalty: $2 500. (10) If— (a) the driver's licence of a holder of an instructors licence or person appointed as an authorised examiner is cancelled; or (b) a holder of an instructors licence or person appointed as an authorised examiner is disqualified from holding or obtaining a driver's licence, the instructors licence or appointment ceases to have any force or effect when the cancellation of the driver's licence, or the disqualification from holding or obtaining a driver's licence, takes effect. (11) If the driver's licence of a holder of an instructors licence or person appointed as an authorised examiner is suspended, the instructors licence or appointment is by force of this section suspended for the same period as the suspension of the driver's licence. (12) An instructors licence or appointment that is suspended by or under this section has no force or effect during the period of the suspension. (13) The powers conferred by this section may be exercised in relation to conduct occurring before or after the commencement of this Part. (14) No civil liability attaches to the Registrar or the Crown in respect of the exercise of a power in good faith under this section. (15) In this section— a person appointed as an authorised examiner means a person appointed as an authorised examiner under section 98AAS. Division 8—Review by Tribunal under this Part 98AAW—Review by Tribunal under this Part (1) A person who is dissatisfied with a reviewable decision of the Registrar, may seek a review of the decision by the Tribunal under section 34 of the South Australian Civil and Administrative Tribunal Act 2013. (2) Subject to subsection (3), an application for review must be made within 1 month of the making of the reviewable decision of the Registrar (or such longer period as the Tribunal may allow). (3) If the reasons of the Registrar are not given in writing at the time of making the reviewable decision and the person (within 1 month of the making of the decision) requires the Registrar to state the reasons in writing, the time for making an application for review of the reviewable decision runs from the time at which the person receives the written statement of those reasons. (4) In this section— reviewable decision means a decision of the Registrar— (a) to refuse to grant or renew, or to suspend or cancel, an instructors licence; (b) to refuse to appoint a person as an authorised examiner, or to suspend or cancel their appointment; (c) to impose a condition on, or vary or revoke a condition of, an instructors licence or a person's appointment as an authorised examiner.
citation: Motor Vehicles (Motor Driving Instructors and Authorised Examiners) Amendment Bill 2024 (SA)

text: Division 9—Miscellaneous 98AAX—Audits (1) It is a condition of every designated authorisation that a person who holds a designated authorisation must— (a) allow a person authorised by the Registrar for the purpose (an auditor) to audit the activities of the person under the authorisation; and (b) adequately address any matter identified by an auditor during the course of an audit under this section. (2) An audit may be conducted by the auditor doing any 1 or more of the following: (a) analysing activities, processes and procedures that have been employed by a person who holds a designated authorisation to ensure compliance with the requirements under this Act and the conditions of their designated authorisation; (b) being present in a motor vehicle while— (i) in the case of a person who holds a designated authorisation that is an instructors licence—teaches another person to drive the vehicle; or (ii) in the case of a person who holds a designated authorisation that is an authorised examiner—conducts a test; (c) examining any vehicle, equipment, designated device (within the meaning of section 98AAP) or other device used by a person who holds a designated authorisation; (d) examining random or selective samples of documents or other records to check on processes and procedures or to ascertain any other relevant matter; (e) conducting interviews of persons who may be able to provide information relevant to the audit; (f) taking such other steps or making such other inquiries as the auditor thinks fit. (3) A person who holds a designated authorisation must, at any time and in the manner determined by the Registrar, provide evidence of compliance with the condition of their designated authorisation applying under this section (as relevant). (4) If— (a) the conduct of a test by an authorised examiner is audited under this section; and (b) the auditor disagrees with the decision of the authorised examiner as to whether the person who undertook the test passed or failed the test, the auditor must advise the Registrar whether, in the opinion of the auditor, the person passed or failed the test. (5) If, during an audit of the holder of a designated authorisation, the auditor identifies, in the holder's undertaking of activities under their designated authorisation, any contravention of this Act or a condition of their designated authorisation in a significant respect or to a significant degree, the auditor must report that contravention to the Registrar. (6) If the Registrar receives advice or a report from an auditor under this section, the Registrar may, after taking such action as the Registrar thinks fit, do 1 or more of the following: (a) make a recommendation to a person who holds a designated authorisation; (b) give a direction to a person who holds a designated authorisation to rectify any matter or take any other action; (c) take disciplinary action under section 98AAV against a person who holds a designated authorisation. (7) If— (a) the Registrar makes a recommendation under subsection (6) to a person who holds a designated authorisation; and (b) the Registrar subsequently considers that the person has not, within a period specified by the Registrar, taken appropriate action in accordance with the recommendation, the Registrar may give a direction to the person. (8) It is a condition of every designated authorisation that the person who holds the designated authorisation must not contravene a direction given to the person under this section. (9) In this section— designated authorisation means— (a) an instructors licence; or (b) an authorised examiner appointment; test means— (a) a practical driving test; or (b) any other assessment or examination. 98AAY—Evidentiary provisions (1) A document purporting to be a certificate signed by the Registrar stating that— (a) a specified person was or was not the holder of an instructors licence or an appointment as an authorised examiner on a specified day; or (b) a specified person was or was not the holder of an instructors licence or an appointment as an authorised examiner of a specified class or subject to specified conditions on a specified day; or (c) a specified device, equipment or vehicle fitting (such as a camera or GPS tracking device) was or was not in use in a specified motor vehicle at a specified time, is proof of the matter so stated in the absence of proof to the contrary. (2) A document purporting to be an extract from, or copy of, an entry contained in a register kept by the Registrar in accordance with section 98AAQ and purporting to be certified as such an extract or copy by the Registrar is, in all legal proceedings, admissible as evidence, and is, in the absence of proof to the contrary, proof of the matters stated without the production of any register or other document upon which any entry may be founded. 98A—Freedom of information The Freedom of Information Act 1991 does not apply in relation to a document (within the meaning of that Act) that is produced by, or provided, downloaded, uploaded or stored from, a designated device (within the meaning of section 98AAP). 13—Amendment of section 98MD—Only persons directed by police to proceed to or be present at scene of accident for purposes related to removal, wrecking or repair (1) Section 98MD(3)—delete "A police officer or an authorised officer" and substitute: An authorised officer (2) Section 98MD(3)—delete "police officer or" 14—Amendment of section 98ME—Towing of vehicle at or from scene of accident (1) Section 98ME(1)(d)—delete "or police officer " (2) Section 98ME(10)—delete "or a police officer" (3) Section 98ME(10)—delete "or police officer" (4) Section 98ME(11)—delete "or a police officer" (5) Section 98ME(13)(b)—delete "or police officer" 15—Amendment of section 98ML—Towtruck driver to carry and produce certificate Section 98ML(b)(i)—delete subparagraph (i) 16—Insertion of Part 4B After section 134M insert: Part 4B—Enforcement Division 1—Appointment of authorised officers 134N—Appointment of authorised officers (1) The Minister may, by instrument in writing, appoint— (a) a specified person to be an authorised officer; or (b) persons of a specified class to be authorised officers. (2) An appointment under subsection (1) may be made subject to conditions or limitations specified in the instrument of appointment. (3) The Minister may, at any time— (a) vary or revoke an appointment; or (b) vary or revoke a condition of an appointment or impose a further condition. 134O—Identity cards (1) Subject to this section, the Minister must issue an authorised officer with an identity card. (2) The Minister is not required to issue an identity card if— (a) the authorised officer is a police officer; or (b) the Minister has designated a card issued to an authorised officer, or to a class of authorised officers, by another person, body or authority (whether or not of this State) as an identity card for the purposes of this Act. (3) An identity card issued under this section must— (a) contain a recent photograph of the authorised officer; and (b) identify the person as an authorised officer for the purposes of this Act; and (c) specify the identification number issued to the authorised officer by the Minister. (4) A police officer who is exercising, or about to exercise, a power is required to comply with a request to identify themself, by— (a) producing the officer's warrant card or other official police identification; or (b) stating orally or in writing the officer's surname, rank and identification number. (5) An authorised officer (other than a police officer) must, at the request of a person in relation to whom the authorised officer intends to exercise powers under this Act, produce for the inspection of the person their identity card. (6) An authorised officer is required to comply with a requirement under subsection (4) or (5)— (a) immediately; or (b) if it is not practicable to comply with the requirement immediately—as soon as practicable afterwards. (7) An authorised officer need only identify themself once to a particular person during the course of an incident, even though more than 1 power is being exercised during the course of the incident. (8) An authorised officer appointed under this Act must, on ceasing to be an authorised officer for any reason, immediately return their identity card to the Minister. Maximum penalty: $5 000. (9) Subsection (8) does not apply if the person has a reasonable excuse. (10) In this section— incident means— (a) a single incident; or (b) a connected series of incidents involving the same or substantially the same parties and occurring during a period of 72 hours; request, in relation to the exercise of a power, means a request made by a person (if any) in respect of whom the power is being or is about to be exercised. Division 2—Powers of authorised officers 134P—General powers of authorised officers (1) Subject to this Act, an authorised officer may, as may be reasonably required for the administration or enforcement of this Act— (a) enter and inspect any place or vehicle or anything on or in the place or vehicle; and (b) give directions with respect to the stopping or moving of a vehicle; and (c) require a vehicle to be presented for inspection at a specified place and time; and (d) require a person to provide data, images, sound or film associated with the use of a specified device, equipment or vehicle fitting (such as a camera or GPS tracking device) or any other device for analysis; and (e) examine, test or weigh any vehicle, equipment or other device, or cause or require it to be examined or tested, or seize it or require its production for examination or testing; and (f) require a person to produce any documents, including a written record that reproduces in an understandable form information stored by computer, microfilm, mobile phone, or other process; and (g) examine, copy or take extracts from a document or information so produced or require a person to provide a copy of any such document or information; and (h) take photographs, films or audio, video or other recordings; and (i) seize and retain, or issue a seizure order in respect of, anything that the authorised officer reasonably suspects has been used in, or may constitute evidence of, a contravention of this Act; and (j) require a person who the authorised officer reasonably suspects has committed, is committing, or is about to commit, a contravention of this Act to state the person's full name and usual place of residence and to produce evidence of the person's identity and, if relevant to the contravention, the person's age; and (k) require a person who the authorised officer reasonably suspects has knowledge of matters in respect of which information is required for the administration or enforcement of this Act to answer questions in relation to those matters; and (l) require, by written notice, that a person attend at the time and place specified in the notice in order to answer questions; and (m) require a person holding or required to hold a permit, licence, accreditation or other authority to produce it for inspection; and (n) give a direction required in connection with the exercise of a power conferred by a paragraph above or otherwise in connection with the administration or enforcement of this Act. (2) An authorised officer may not exercise the power of entry under this section in respect of premises unless the premises are business premises being used at the time in the course of business. (3) An authorised officer may not exercise the power under this section to enter, inspect or seize a vehicle unless— (a) the vehicle is of a prescribed class; or (b) the authorised officer reasonably suspects that— (i) a contravention of this Act has been, is being, or is about to be, committed in relation to the vehicle; or (ii) something may be found in or on the vehicle that has been used in, or constitutes evidence of, a contravention of this Act. (4) In the exercise of powers under this Act, an authorised officer may be assisted by such persons as the authorised officer considers necessary in the circumstances. (5) An authorised officer may require an occupier of a place or a person apparently in charge of any vehicle or other thing to give to the authorised officer, or a person assisting the authorised officer, such assistance as is reasonably required by the authorised officer for the effective exercise of powers conferred by this Act. (6) If a time and place specified in a notice under subsection (1)(l) is not reasonable in the circumstances, the authorised officer is to nominate a time and place after taking into account any request of the person who is the subject of the notice. 134Q—Provisions relating to warrants (1) If, on the application of an authorised officer, a magistrate is satisfied that there are reasonable grounds to believe— (a) that a contravention of this Act has been, is being, or is about to be, committed in or on any place or a vehicle; or (b) that something may be found in or on a place that has been used in, or constitutes evidence of, a contravention of this Act; or (c) that the circumstances require immediate action, the magistrate may issue a warrant in respect of the place or vehicle authorising an authorised officer, with such assistants as the authorised officer considers necessary, to use reasonable force to break into or open any part of, or anything in or on, the place or vehicle as specified in the warrant. (2) An application for the issue of a warrant under this section— (a) may be made either personally or by telephone; and (b) must be made in accordance with any procedures prescribed by the regulations. (3) A warrant, if not executed at the expiration of 1 month from the date of its issue, then expires. 134R—Provisions relating to seizure (1) A seizure order— (a) must be in the form of a notice given in the prescribed manner to the owner or person in control of the thing to which the order relates; and (b) may be varied or revoked by further notice given in the prescribed manner. (2) If a seizure order is issued, a person who removes or interferes with the thing to which the order relates without the approval of the Minister before an order is made under subsection (3)(b) in respect of the thing or the seizure order is discharged under subsection (3)(c) is guilty of an offence.
citation: Motor Vehicles (Motor Driving Instructors and Authorised Examiners) Amendment Bill 2024 (SA)

text: Maximum penalty: $10 000. (3) If a thing has been seized or made subject to a seizure order, the following provisions apply: (a) the thing must, if it has been seized, be held pending proceedings for an offence against this Act related to the thing seized, unless the Minister, on application, authorises its release to the person from whom it was seized, or to any person who had legal title to it at the time of its seizure, subject to such conditions as the Minister thinks fit (including conditions as to the giving of security for satisfaction of an order under paragraph (b)(ii)); (b) if proceedings for an offence against this Act relating to the thing are instituted within the prescribed period after its seizure or the issuing of the seizure order and the defendant is convicted or found guilty of the offence, the court may— (i) order that it be forfeited to the Minister; or (ii) where it has been released pursuant to paragraph (a) or is the subject of a seizure order—order that it be forfeited to the Minister or that the person to whom it was released or the defendant pay to the Minister an amount equal to its market value at the time of its seizure or the issuing of the seizure order, as the court thinks fit; (c) if — (i) proceedings are not instituted for an offence against this Act relating to the thing within the prescribed period after its seizure or the issuing of the seizure order; or (ii) proceedings have been so instituted and— (A) the defendant is found not guilty of the offence; or (B) the defendant is convicted or found guilty of the offence but no order for forfeiture is made under paragraph (b), then— (iii) in the case of a thing seized—the person from whom the thing was seized, or any person with legal title to it, is entitled to recover from the Minister (if necessary, by action in a court of competent jurisdiction) the thing itself, or if it has been damaged or destroyed, compensation of an amount equal to its market value at the time of its seizure; or (iv) in the case of a thing subject to a seizure order—the order is discharged. (4) In subsection (3)— the prescribed period means 12 months or such longer period as the Magistrates Court may, on application by the Minister, allow. 134S—Offences against authorised officers A person who— (a) without reasonable excuse hinders or obstructs an authorised officer; or (b) fails to answer a question put by an authorised officer to the best of the person's knowledge, information or belief; or (c) produces a document or record that the person knows, or ought to know, is false or misleading in a material particular; or (d) being the person in charge of a place or vehicle subject to an inspection and having been required to provide reasonable assistance to facilitate the inspection, refuses or fails to provide such assistance; or (e) fails without reasonable excuse to comply with a requirement or direction of an authorised officer under this Act; or (f) uses abusive, threatening or insulting language to an authorised officer, or a person assisting an authorised officer; or (g) falsely represents, by words or conduct, that the person is an authorised officer, is guilty of an offence. Maximum penalty: $20 000. 134T—Self‑incrimination If a person is required to answer a question or to produce, or provide a copy of, a document or information under this Act and the answer, document or information would tend to incriminate the person or make the person liable to a penalty, the person must nevertheless answer or produce, or provide a copy of, the document or information, but the answer, document or information will not (except as provided in section 98AAP(7)(c)) be admissible in evidence against the person in proceedings for an offence other than proceedings in respect of the making of a false or misleading statement or declaration. 134U—Interaction of this Division with Part 2 Division 5 of Road Traffic Act 1961 The provisions of this Division are in addition to, and do not derogate from, the provisions of Part 2 Division 5 of the Road Traffic Act 1961. 17—Amendment of section 137—Duty to answer certain questions Section 137—delete "a police officer or" 18—Amendment of section 138A—Commissioner of Police to give certain information to Registrar Section 138A—after "permit" insert: , appointment as an authorised examiner under Part 3A 19—Amendment of section 139—Inspection of motor vehicles (1) Section 139(4)(b),(5) and (8)—delete ", a police officer" wherever occurring (2) Section 139(15), definition of authorised vehicle inspector, (b)—delete paragraph (b) 20—Amendment of section 143B—General defences Section 143B(1)(b)—delete "or police officer" 21—Amendment of section 145—Regulations and fee notices (1) Section 145(1)(c)—delete "instructor's licence" and substitute: motor driving instructors licence under Part 3A (2) Section 145(1)(e)—after "for" second occurring insert: or for the purposes of (3) Section 145(1)—after paragraph (gf) insert: (gi) providing for saving or transitional provisions consequent on the enactment of the Motor Vehicles (Motor Driving Instructors and Authorised Examiners) Amendment Act 2024 (being provisions additional to those set out in Schedule 1 of that Act); and (4) Section 145—after subsection (1b) insert: (1c) Regulations made under subsection (1)(gi) may, if the regulation so provides, take effect from the commencement of the amending Act or from a later day. (1d) Despite any other Act or law, no compensation is payable by the Crown in respect of the operation of regulations made under subsection (1)(gi). (1e) Regulations under this Act may provide that a fee prescribed for the purposes of this subsection paid to the Registrar is not refundable. Schedule 1—Transitional provisions 1—Interpretation In this Part— commencement day means the day on which section 12 of this Act comes into operation; new Part 3A means Part 3A of the principal Act as inserted by section 12 of this Act; principal Act means the Motor Vehicles Act 1959; Registrar has the same meaning as in the principal Act; relevant day means the day that falls 12 months after the commencement day or such later day as may be declared by the Registrar by notice in the Gazette. 2—Applications for motor driving instructors' licences under Motor Vehicles Act 1959 (1) Despite section 98A of the principal Act (as in force before the commencement of this clause), an application for the issue of a class C motor driving instructor's licence under that section cannot be made on or after the declared day (and such a class C motor driving instructor's licence cannot be granted on or after the declared day in respect of such an application). (2) Subclause (1) does not apply in relation to an application made by the holder of a class C motor driving instructor's licence for the issue of a further class C motor driving instructor's licence to the holder to take effect on the expiration of an earlier licence. (3) In this clause— class C motor driving instructor's licence means a motor driving instructor's licence issued to a person under section 98A of the principal Act in force before the commencement of this clause that only permits the person to teach another person to drive a motor vehicle authorised to be driven under a licence assigned the C classification; declared day means the day declared by the Registrar by notice in the Gazette (being a day that falls before the commencement day). 3—Current motor driving instructor's licence holders (1) The following provisions apply in relation to a person who holds a motor driving instructor's licence in force under section 98A of the principal Act immediately before the commencement day: (a) on the commencement day, the person will be taken to be a motor driving instructor's licence holder under the principal Act; (b) the licence that the person holds will be taken to be a motor driving instructors licence under new Part 3A (a deemed licence) subject to the same terms and conditions as those applying to the person's motor driving instructor's licence under section 98A of the principal Act immediately before the commencement day; (c) new Part 3A applies to a deemed licence as if it were a licence granted under new Part 3A. (2) Despite subclause (1)— (a) a person's deemed licence will have effect— (i) if the person applies for and is granted a motor driving instructors licence under new Part 3A to replace the deemed licence—until the day on which the licence granted under new Part 3A takes effect; or (ii) in any other case—until the relevant day (unless the deemed licence is cancelled before that day under another provision of new Part 3A); (b) the Registrar may, at any time, by notice given to the person as the Registrar thinks fit— (i) impose additional conditions on the deemed licence; or (ii) substitute, vary or revoke a condition of the deemed licence, (and the person's deemed licence will be taken to be subject to those conditions for the purposes of the principal Act). (3) The repeal of section 98A of the principal Act by this Act does not affect any action, investigation or other process commenced under that section before the commencement day in relation to a person, and the outcome of any such process may have effect for the purposes of any licence held by the person under new Part 3A, including a deemed licence. (4) If the Registrar grants a person a motor driving instructors licence under new Part 3A to replace a deemed licence, in determining the period for which the licence is to be in force, the Registrar may take into account the balance of the term that was remaining on the deemed licence. (5) To avoid doubt, a deemed licence that ceases to have effect on the relevant day under this clause is cancelled by force of this clause on the relevant day. 4—Current authorised examiners (1) An authorised examiner appointment in force immediately before the commencement day under paragraph (a) of the definition of authorised examiner in section 5(1) of the principal Act continues in force after the commencement day. (2) The following provisions apply in relation to a person who holds an authorised examiner appointment (not being a designated authorised examiner appointment) in force immediately before the commencement day under paragraph (b) of the definition of authorised examiner in section 5(1) of the principal Act: (a) on the commencement day, the person will be taken to be appointed as an authorised examiner under new Part 3A; (b) the appointment that the person holds will be taken to be an authorised examiner appointment under new Part 3A (a deemed authorised examiner appointment) subject to the same terms and conditions as those applying to the person's authorised examiner appointment (if any) under the principal Act immediately before the commencement day; (c) new Part 3A applies to a deemed authorised examiner appointment as if it were an appointment made under new Part 3A. (3) Despite subclause (1)— (a) a person's deemed appointment will have effect— (i) if the person is appointed as an authorised examiner under new Part 3A to replace the deemed appointment—until the day on which the appointment made under new Part 3A takes effect; or (ii) in any other case—until the relevant day (unless the deemed appointment is cancelled before that day under another provision of new Part 3A); and (b) the Registrar may, at any time, by notice given to the person as the Registrar thinks fit— (i) impose conditions (or additional conditions) on the deemed appointment; or (ii) substitute, vary or revoke a condition of the deemed appointment, (and the person's deemed appointment will be taken to be subject to those conditions for the purposes of the principal Act). (4) A designated authorised examiner appointment in force immediately before the commencement day under paragraph (b) of the definition of authorised examiner in section 5(1) of the principal Act is cancelled by force of this clause on the commencement day. (5) Any action, investigation or other process commenced under the principal Act before the commencement day in relation to a person holding an appointment as an authorised examiner under the principal Act (as in force immediately before the commencement day), and the outcome of any such action, investigation or other process is not affected by the amendments to the principal Act by sections 3 and 11 of this Act and may have effect for the purposes of any appointment held by the person under new Part 3A, including a deemed appointment. (6) If the Registrar appoints a person to be an authorised examiner under new Part 3A to replace a deemed authorised examiner appointment, in determining the period for which the appointment is to be in force, the Registrar may take into account the balance of the term that was remaining on the deemed authorised examiner appointment. (7) To avoid doubt, a deemed authorised examiner appointment that ceases to have effect on the relevant day under this clause is cancelled by force of this clause on the relevant day. (8) In this clause— designated authorised examiner appointment means an authorised examiner appointment that only allows the authorised examiner the subject of the appointment to conduct practical driving tests for another person seeking to be granted a licence assigned the C classification. 5—Crown not liable to pay compensation Despite any other Act or law, no compensation is payable by the Crown in respect of the operation of this Schedule.
